statement: Residential 120/240 V service uses a center-tapped transformer winding with the neutral as the midpoint, so each leg sits at 120 V relative to neutral. If the service neutral is lost (broken or loose connection), the 240 V across the two legs no longer divides evenly: a heavily loaded leg collapses toward 80 V while the lightly loaded leg spikes toward 160 V, damaging equipment on the rising leg. The neutral-to-ground bond at the service provides a backup return path through the grounding electrode system that partially stabilizes the system when the service neutral is compromised.
